findings.diff_summary: ONE line changed (line 113): replaced inline `rm -f '/tmp/claude-close-force-<DEV_SESSION_ID>.flag' 2>/dev/null || true` shell snippet with a reference to `scripts/cleanup-close-force-sentinel.sh` with the resolved sentinel path. Change reduces verbosity marginally; no new verbose section added.
findings.recommendation: No action required. The changed line is procedural and marginally less verbose than its predecessor. The large Workflow section (lines 32-377, ~346 lines) is pre-existing command execution specification: argument parsing, forced-override path, task-id resolution, artifact preflight, step dispatch, verdict branches. No critical verbose patterns detected (no Philosophy, Overview, Quality Standards, Safety Features, or Helper Scripts sections).

Functional shell script (13 lines, new file). Outside prompt-inspector command/agent documentation scope. Shell scripts do not contain verbose section headers (Philosophy, Overview, etc.). No prompt-verbosity finding emitted; introduced_in_diff is not applicable because there is no finding object.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex confirmed the close.md conclusion is substantively correct (OBSERVATION_ONLY). Three PROPOSED_FIX items were returned: (1) summary accounting should distinguish listed/in-scope-changed-prompt-docs/skipped-no-diff/skipped-out-of-scope counts rather than a flat files_inspected count; (2) do.md should be marked skipped-no-diff rather than inspected-no-violation; (3) cleanup script should use correct line count (13, not 14) and clearly be marked out-of-scope with no finding object and introduced_in_diff: null.

findings.rationale: Codex's proposed summary structure is more precise and correctly reflects that do.md and the shell script were not inspected for findings. Applied in summary section with the more descriptive field names.
